fields.description:
## What
One-time backfill of the complexity and clone-count series by checking out sampled historical commits, running the metric tools, and writing snapshots into the going-forward store so those two trends have history.

## Files
- `scripts/backfill_complexity_clone_snapshots.py` — new: for sampled commits (default monthly), measure the tree and append snapshots via `src/rebar/metrics/snapshot.py`.
  - **Complexity tool + parse:** `lizard <src_dir>` (cyclomatic complexity; already a project dependency-of-record for grounding, see `src/rebar/grounding/engine_b_backends.py`). Parse its per-function CCN and record `complexity` = the SUMMED cyclomatic complexity across all functions (an `int`).
  - **Clone tool + parse:** `jscpd --reporters json --output <tmpdir> <src_dir>`; read `<tmpdir>/jscpd-report.json` and record `clone_count` = `statistics.total.clones` (the number of duplicated blocks, an `int`).
  - **Measurement seam:** the per-commit measurement is a single function `measure_tree(tree_path) -> {"complexity": int, "clone_count": int}` that shells to the two tools; the test monkeypatches THIS function to return stub values, so the test needs neither `lizard` nor `jscpd` installed. A tool that is absent or errors makes `measure_tree` return `None` and the commit is skipped (logged, non-fatal).
  - **`ts` MUST be the sampled commit's date**, not wall-clock: `write_snapshot(record, ts=<commit ISO date>, repo_root=...)` where the date is `git show -s --format=%cI <commit>` — otherwise every backfilled point would collapse onto the backfill-run time and the trend would be meaningless.
- `tests/scripts/test_backfill_complexity_clone.py` — new.

## Test tier
The test lives under `tests/scripts/` with `pytestmark = pytest.mark.scripts` (the scripts tier; `tests/unit/scripts/` does NOT exist). External tools are stubbed/mocked so the test is deterministic and needs no scc/lizard/jscpd installed.

## Approach
Complexity/clone are point-in-time; their movement needs seeded history, which git makes deterministic (checkout + tool run at each sampled commit). Writes through the same `snapshot.py` writer as the going-forward path so backfilled and live snapshots share one format; snapshots are labeled `source=snapshot, confidence=high` (deterministic tool output). Sampling cadence is a script arg (default monthly) — a stated default. This is an optional backfill script the core command does not depend on.

## Tests
- Unit (temp git repo + stubbed tool output): the script writes one snapshot per sampled commit with the parsed complexity/clone numbers; a commit whose tool run fails is skipped and logged, not fatal.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/scripts/test_backfill_complexity_clone.py` passes (tier `scripts`): with `measure_tree` stubbed, sampling 3 fixture commits writes 3 snapshots, each containing integer `complexity` and `clone_count` keys, and each tagged with `ts` equal to its COMMIT's date (not the run time) — asserted by reading the snapshots back via `read_snapshots` and checking the per-commit `ts` and int-typed values.
- [ ] a sampled commit whose `measure_tree` returns `None` (tool absent/errors) is skipped without aborting the backfill — test asserts the run continues and the other snapshots are still written.
- [ ] every written snapshot carries `source="snapshot"`, `confidence="high"` — asserted.
